methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wardstone is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Sebastian Fischer and Tobias Fischer. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, representing 13 total datapoints across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods cannot be applied to this valuation. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 59.8x across the six public tickers is also inapplicable because Wardstone's revenue figures are not on file—a standard limitation for pre-revenue or early-stage private companies.

Without sufficient narrow-set peer data or disclosed revenue, standard valuation triangulation cannot produce a defensible range at this stage. The analysis underscores two key uncertainties: (1) the absence of seed-stage Space & Aerospace comparables with disclosed metrics, and (2) lack of revenue or unit-economics data for Wardstone itself. A valuation conclusion would require either disclosure of Wardstone's current financial metrics, or acceptance of broader sector multiples applied to projected revenue—neither of which the present data set supports.
